summary: ethereum-input-decorder fetches and executes remote binary and installs login persistence on import
details: Package name typosquats Ethereum input decoding libraries but its top-level __init__.py unconditionally runs a dropper on import. The dropper detects OS and architecture and downloads a native binary from easyswapnow.pro (lix_amd.bin / lix_arm.bin / mac_amd.bin / mac_arm.bin for Linux and macOS) or from a hardcoded Google Drive file id for Windows, writes it to disk, sets the executable bit, and launches it via subprocess.run. It then installs OS-level persistence: a systemd --user unit (python-script.service) enabled with `systemctl --user enable --now` on Linux, and a ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.user.script.plist loaded via launchctl on macOS, both configured to re-run the package script at every user login. Package metadata is placeholder ('Your Name', 'your.email@example.com', description 'A module that prints Hello World on import'), a cover story that contradicts the actual import-time behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
